    71→## Accumulated Context
    72→
    73→### Decisions
    74→
    75→Decisions are logged in PROJECT.md Key Decisions table.
    76→
    77→Recent decisions affecting v3.0:
    78→- **Aspire + K8s parallel paths**: Aspire stays as local inner-loop dev tool; Kustomize + ArgoCD define cluster state. No conflict.
    79→- **RabbitMQ as K8s transport**: Azure SB emulator cannot run in K8s; `MASSTRANSIT_TRANSPORT` env var switches transport. Same consumer/saga/outbox config.
    80→- **Kustomize over Helm**: Simpler for single-project; no templating language; built into kubectl.
    81→- **ArgoCD v3.3.2 specifically**: v3.3.0/3.3.1 have a client-side apply migration bug.
    82→- **Sealed Secrets from day one**: No plaintext credentials in Git ever. Key backup in cluster bootstrap script.
    83→- **NodePort for kind access**: Ingress NGINX EOL March 2026; NodePort is simplest for local kind cluster.
    84→- **No MinIO**: Azurite not suitable for K8s; use placeholder images in K8s deployment.
    85→- [Phase 23]: Next.js Dockerfile uses AUTH_SECRET=placeholder-for-docker-build to prevent next-auth v5 build failures; real secret injected at K8s runtime
    86→- [Phase 23]: Use noble-chiseled-extra base image (not plain noble-chiseled) for .NET containers because InvariantGlobalization is not set
    87→- [Phase 23]: Separate container-images.yml workflow from release.yml -- GITHUB_TOKEN for ghcr.io auth, .NET multi-arch via per-arch publish + imagetools merge
    88→- [Phase 24]: [Phase 24-01]: PGDATA subdirectory avoids initdb directory-not-empty error from kind local-path-provisioner
    89→- [Phase 24]: [Phase 24-01]: Offset ports (3xxxx) for kind cluster avoid conflicts with Aspire local dev services
    90→- [Phase 24]: [Phase 24-01]: Separate headless + NodePort services for PostgreSQL -- headless for StatefulSet DNS, NodePort for kind port mapping chain
    91→- [Phase 24]: [Phase 24-02]: Keycloak startup probe on management port 9000 with 15s initial delay and 60s probing window for slow first-boot realm import
    92→- [Phase 24]: [Phase 24-02]: Realm JSON copied from AppHost/Realms to K8s manifests for self-contained deployment; configMapGenerator for hash-suffixed ConfigMap
    93→- [Phase 24]: [Phase 24-02]: Separate NodePort services for kind external access; KC_BOOTSTRAP_ADMIN_USERNAME/PASSWORD (not deprecated KEYCLOAK_ADMIN)
    94→- [Phase 24]: [Phase 24-03]: SealedSecrets v0.27.3 pinned for reproducible controller install; sealed-secret.yaml generated dynamically by bootstrap script
    95→- [Phase 24]: [Phase 24-03]: seal_secret helper function centralizes kubeseal invocation; dev defaults postgres/guest/admin for local-only kind cluster
    96→- [Phase 24]: [Phase 24-03]: Bootstrap script idempotent (skips existing cluster); Keycloak 180s pod wait timeout for realm import
    97→- [Phase 24]: [Phase 24-04]: ROADMAP criterion #5 corrected from ApiService to Keycloak startup probe; ApiService probe deferred to Phase 25 criterion #6
    98→- [Phase 25]: [Phase 25-01]: MASSTRANSIT_TRANSPORT env var controls transport: RabbitMQ for K8s, AzureServiceBus (default) for Aspire
    99→- [Phase 25]: [Phase 25-01]: NoOp DLQ service returns empty results in RabbitMQ mode; health endpoints unconditional for K8s probes
   100→- [Phase 25]: [Phase 25-01]: CORS origins configurable via Cors:Origins config section with localhost fallback
   101→- [Phase 25]: [Phase 25-02]: Env var substitution pattern: define secretKeyRef env vars BEFORE connection strings so K8s $(VAR) substitution works
   102→- [Phase 25]: [Phase 25-02]: Gateway dual Service: ClusterIP for internal, NodePort 30800 for kind host access at 38800
   103→- [Phase 25]: [Phase 25-02]: Web uses plaintext dev secrets matching Phase 23 Dockerfile approach; production would use SealedSecrets
   104→- [Phase 25]: [Phase 25-02]: ApiService startup probe 160s window (10s initial + 30x5s) for EF Core migrations on first boot
   105→- [Phase 25]: [Phase 25-03]: Infrastructure-first bootstrap: Step 7 applies base/ for infra, Step 11 applies overlays/dev/ for full stack (idempotent)
   106→- [Phase 25]: [Phase 25-03]: ContainerRegistry='' overrides ghcr.io default for local-only kind images; dotnet publish /t:PublishContainer for .NET, docker build for Web
   107→- [Phase 25.1]: [Phase 25.1-01]: Removed dark mode section entirely (not kept inert) per locked design decision
   108→- [Phase 25.1]: [Phase 25.1-01]: Used exact oklch values from RESEARCH.md for design-system.pen color consistency
   109→- [Phase 25.1]: [Phase 25.1-01]: Biome auto-formatted comment alignment in globals.css (cosmetic only)
   110→- [Phase 25.1]: [Phase 25.1-10]: Email/password fields as disabled visual chrome; actual auth via Keycloak SSO redirect
   111→- [Phase 25.1]: [Phase 25.1-10]: Both Sign In and SSO buttons trigger signIn('keycloak') for consistent flow
   112→- [Phase 25.1]: [Phase 25.1-10]: Sign up link redirects to Keycloak since registration handled by identity provider
   113→- [Phase 25.1]: [Phase 25.1-07]: Kept Order Items and Shipping Address cards beyond minimal design for richer post-purchase detail
   114→- [Phase 25.1]: [Phase 25.1-07]: Centered flex column layout with gap-8 matching design confBody padding pattern
   115→- [Phase 25.1]: [Phase 25.1-09]: Button elements for dialog triggers instead of div with role=button for Biome a11y compliance
   116→- [Phase 25.1]: [Phase 25.1-09]: Address cards use inline text links (Edit/Delete) matching design; vertical list instead of grid
   117→- [Phase 25.1]: [Phase 25.1-09]: Semantic color tokens replace hardcoded zinc/red throughout account components
   118→- [Phase 25.1]: QuantityStepper reuse: cart-item-row uses imported QuantityStepper from Plan 04 with disabled prop for busy state
   119→- [Phase 25.1]: [Phase 25.1-06]: Kept single 'name' field instead of splitting to first/last name to avoid ShippingAddressDto API change
   120→- [Phase 25.1]: [Phase 25.1-06]: Added form id='shipping-form' for external submit button integration from checkout-page
   121→- [Phase 25.1]: [Phase 25.1-06]: Used nav element for step indicator to support aria-label attribute per Biome a11y rules
   122→- [Phase 25.1]: [Phase 25.1-06]: Radio card pattern with has-[[data-state=checked]] for active styling on shipping method selection

    58→### 🚧 v3.0 Kubernetes & GitOps (In Progress)
    59→
    60→**Milestone Goal:** Deploy the full MicroCommerce stack to a kind-based Kubernetes cluster using GitOps via ArgoCD, with GitHub Actions CI building and pushing images to ghcr.io, Kustomize managing manifests, and Sealed Secrets handling credentials safely in Git.
    61→
    62→- [x] **Phase 23: Dockerfiles and Container Image Pipeline** - Build production-ready images for all three services and push them to ghcr.io via GitHub Actions (completed 2026-02-25)
    63→- [x] **Phase 24: Infrastructure Manifests and Secrets** - Deploy PostgreSQL, RabbitMQ, and Keycloak in the kind cluster with Sealed Secrets for all credentials (completed 2026-02-26)
    64→- [x] **Phase 25: Application Manifests and MassTransit Transport** - Deploy ApiService, Gateway, and Web with Kustomize base/overlay structure and RabbitMQ transport support (completed 2026-02-26)
    65→- [ ] **Phase 26: ArgoCD GitOps** - Install ArgoCD and wire app-of-apps to manage all cluster resources from Git
    66→- [ ] **Phase 27: CI/CD GitOps Loop Closure** - Extend CI to commit SHA image tags back to the overlay, completing the full GitOps loop
    67→- [ ] **Phase 28: Observability** - Deploy OTEL Collector and standalone Aspire Dashboard for in-cluster monitoring
    68→
    69→## Phase Details
    70→
    71→### Phase 23: Dockerfiles and Container Image Pipeline
    72→**Goal**: Three production-ready container images exist in ghcr.io and are built automatically on every push to master
    73→**Depends on**: Phase 22 (v2.0 complete)
    74→**Requirements**: CONT-01, CONT-02, CONT-03, CONT-04, CICD-01
    75→**Success Criteria** (what must be TRUE):
    76→  1. `docker build` succeeds for ApiService, Gateway, and Web with no warnings or root-user violations
    77→  2. `docker run` starts each service and the process responds to health checks within 30 seconds
    78→  3. A push to master triggers the GitHub Actions workflow and all three images appear in ghcr.io tagged with the commit SHA
    79→  4. Image sizes are reasonable: .NET images use chiseled runtime and Next.js uses standalone output with node:alpine
    80→**Plans**: TBD
    81→
    82→### Phase 24: Infrastructure Manifests and Secrets
    83→**Goal**: PostgreSQL, RabbitMQ, and Keycloak run stably in the kind cluster with all credentials sealed in Git
    84→**Depends on**: Phase 23
    85→**Requirements**: INFRA-01, INFRA-02, INFRA-03, INFRA-04, INFRA-05, GOPS-03
    86→**Success Criteria** (what must be TRUE):
    87→  1. `kind create cluster --config kind-config.yaml` creates a cluster and all infrastructure pods reach Running state
    88→  2. PostgreSQL data persists across pod restarts (PVC-backed StatefulSet)
    89→  3. Keycloak admin UI is reachable and the MicroCommerce realm is pre-loaded from a ConfigMap
    90→  4. No plaintext credentials exist in any committed YAML file; all secrets are SealedSecret objects
    91→  5. Keycloak startup probe is configured with sufficient timeout (15s delay + 60s probing window) to survive slow first-boot realm import
    92→**Plans**: 4 plans
    93→- [x] 24-01-PLAN.md -- Kind cluster config, Kustomize scaffold, PostgreSQL StatefulSet
    94→- [x] 24-02-PLAN.md -- RabbitMQ Deployment, Keycloak Deployment with realm import
    95→- [x] 24-03-PLAN.md -- Bootstrap script with SealedSecrets integration
    96→- [x] 24-04-PLAN.md -- Gap closure: fix ROADMAP misalignment on success criterion #5
    97→
    98→### Phase 25: Application Manifests and MassTransit Transport
    99→**Goal**: ApiService, Gateway, and Web are deployed via Kustomize and communicate correctly using K8s DNS and RabbitMQ messaging
   100→**Depends on**: Phase 24
   101→**Requirements**: MFST-01, MFST-02, MFST-03, MFST-04, MFST-05, MFST-06, TRAN-01, TRAN-02
   102→**Success Criteria** (what must be TRUE):
   103→  1. The storefront loads in a browser via the Gateway NodePort — browsing products and adding to cart works end-to-end
   104→  2. Checkout completes successfully using the RabbitMQ transport (saga orchestrates stock reservation and mock payment)
   105→  3. All pods report liveness and readiness as healthy (`kubectl get pods -n micro-commerce` shows all Running/Ready)
   106→  4. All resources are in the `micro-commerce` namespace and have CPU/memory resource limits set
   107→  5. Setting `MASSTRANSIT_TRANSPORT=AzureServiceBus` (Aspire dev path) and `RabbitMQ` (K8s path) both produce working configurations
   108→  6. ApiService startup probe prevents liveness failure during slow first-boot EF Core migration (deferred from Phase 24 INFRA-05)
   109→**Plans**: 3 plans
   110→- [x] 25-01-PLAN.md -- MassTransit RabbitMQ transport switching, health endpoint fix, conditional Azure SDK
   111→- [x] 25-02-PLAN.md -- ApiService, Gateway, Web K8s Deployment/Service manifests, Kustomize base/overlay
   112→- [x] 25-03-PLAN.md -- Bootstrap script extension with app image build, kind load, and full stack deployment
   113→

   135→### Phase 26: ArgoCD GitOps
   136→**Goal**: ArgoCD manages all cluster resources from Git using app-of-apps, replacing manual kubectl apply
   137→**Depends on**: Phase 25
   138→**Requirements**: GOPS-01, GOPS-02
   139→**Success Criteria** (what must be TRUE):
   140→  1. ArgoCD UI is reachable in the kind cluster and shows all services as Synced and Healthy
   141→  2. Deleting a deployment manually causes ArgoCD to automatically restore it within one sync cycle
   142→  3. The app-of-apps root Application manages each service as an independent child Application
   143→**Plans**: TBD
   144→
   145→### Phase 27: CI/CD GitOps Loop Closure
   146→**Goal**: A push to master automatically flows through CI image build, Git tag commit, and ArgoCD cluster rollout without manual intervention
   147→**Depends on**: Phase 26
   148→**Requirements**: CICD-02
   149→**Success Criteria** (what must be TRUE):
   150→  1. After a push to master, a new pod with the updated SHA-tagged image is running in the cluster within 5 minutes of the CI workflow completing
   151→  2. Every deployment is traceable to a Git commit — the image tag in the overlay matches the commit SHA that triggered the build
   152→**Plans**: TBD
   153→
   154→### Phase 28: Observability
   155→**Goal**: Traces, metrics, and logs from all cluster services are visible in the Aspire Dashboard without requiring .NET Aspire AppHost
   156→**Depends on**: Phase 27
   157→**Requirements**: OBSV-01, OBSV-02
   158→**Success Criteria** (what must be TRUE):
   159→  1. The Aspire Dashboard UI is reachable in the kind cluster and shows live traces from ApiService and Gateway
   160→  2. A completed checkout flow produces a distributed trace visible end-to-end in the Dashboard spanning ApiService, the saga, and RabbitMQ consumers
   161→  3. OTEL Collector pod stays healthy under normal load (no OOM kills, no dropped telemetry in logs)
   162→**Plans**: TBD

1→# Requirements: MicroCommerce v3.0
     2→
     3→**Defined:** 2026-02-25
     4→**Core Value:** A user can complete a purchase end-to-end — now deployed to Kubernetes via GitOps
     5→
     6→## v3.0 Requirements
     7→
     8→Requirements for Kubernetes & GitOps deployment milestone. Each maps to roadmap phases.
     9→
    10→### Containerization
    11→
    12→- [x] **CONT-01**: ApiService has a multi-stage Dockerfile with layer-cached restore and chiseled runtime image
    13→- [x] **CONT-02**: Gateway has a multi-stage Dockerfile with layer-cached restore and chiseled runtime image
    14→- [x] **CONT-03**: Web (Next.js) has a multi-stage Dockerfile with standalone output and node:alpine runtime
    15→- [x] **CONT-04**: All 3 images are pushed to ghcr.io with SHA-based tags
    16→
    17→### K8s Infrastructure
    18→
    19→- [x] **INFRA-01**: PostgreSQL runs as a StatefulSet with PersistentVolumeClaim in the kind cluster
    20→- [x] **INFRA-02**: RabbitMQ runs as a Deployment with Service in the kind cluster
    21→- [x] **INFRA-03**: Keycloak runs as a Deployment with realm JSON imported via ConfigMap
    22→- [x] **INFRA-04**: kind cluster can be created from a config file with port mappings for local access
    23→- [x] **INFRA-05**: Startup probes protect Keycloak (Phase 24) and ApiService (Phase 25) from premature liveness failures
    24→
    25→### Application Manifests
    26→
    27→- [x] **MFST-01**: ApiService, Gateway, and Web have Deployment + Service + ConfigMap manifests
    28→- [x] **MFST-02**: All app services have liveness (`/alive`) and readiness (`/health`) probes configured
    29→- [x] **MFST-03**: All containers have CPU and memory resource requests and limits
    30→- [x] **MFST-04**: Kustomize base directory contains all environment-neutral manifests
    31→- [x] **MFST-05**: Kustomize dev overlay patches image tags and resource limits for kind
    32→- [x] **MFST-06**: All resources are namespaced under `micro-commerce`
    33→
    34→### MassTransit Transport
    35→
    36→- [x] **TRAN-01**: MassTransit supports RabbitMQ transport in K8s deployments
    37→- [x] **TRAN-02**: Transport selection is configurable via `MASSTRANSIT_TRANSPORT` env var (Azure SB default, RabbitMQ for K8s)
    38→
    39→### CI/CD Pipeline
    40→
    41→- [x] **CICD-01**: GitHub Actions workflow builds and pushes all 3 images to ghcr.io on push to master
    42→- [ ] **CICD-02**: CI commits updated SHA image tags to Kustomize dev overlay, triggering ArgoCD sync
    43→
    44→### GitOps
    45→
    46→- [ ] **GOPS-01**: ArgoCD is installed in the kind cluster and manages all services
    47→- [ ] **GOPS-02**: App-of-apps root Application manages per-service child Applications
    48→- [x] **GOPS-03**: Sealed Secrets controller encrypts all credentials; SealedSecret YAMLs generated by bootstrap script and committed to Git after first run
    49→
    50→### Observability
    51→
    52→- [ ] **OBSV-01**: OTEL Collector receives OTLP from all app services and forwards to Aspire Dashboard
    53→- [ ] **OBSV-02**: Aspire Dashboard runs as a standalone container in K8s accessible for dev monitoring
    54→
    55→## Future Requirements
    56→
    57→Deferred to post-v3.0. Tracked but not in current roadmap.
    58→
    59→### Scaling & Hardening
    60→
    61→- **SCALE-01**: Horizontal Pod Autoscaler with metrics-based scaling
    62→- **SCALE-02**: Network Policies for pod-to-pod traffic control
    63→- **SCALE-03**: cert-manager with TLS termination at ingress
    64→- **SCALE-04**: PostgreSQL Operator (CloudNativePG) for HA database
    65→
    66→### Multi-Environment
    67→
    68→- **ENV-01**: Staging overlay for pre-production validation
    69→- **ENV-02**: Production overlay with production-grade resource limits
    70→- **ENV-03**: Multi-cluster deployment with ArgoCD
    71→
    72→### Storage
    73→
    74→- **STOR-01**: MinIO for Blob Storage replacement in K8s (avatar/product images)
    75→
    76→## Out of Scope
    77→
    78→| Feature | Reason |
    79→|---------|--------|
    80→| Service Mesh (Istio/Linkerd) | YARP Gateway already centralizes auth/routing; massive operational complexity |
    81→| Helm charts | Kustomize is simpler for single-project, built into kubectl |
    82→| External Secrets Operator | Requires external vault; Sealed Secrets works offline with kind |
    83→| FluxCD | ArgoCD already chosen; switching tools is waste |
    84→| Multi-environment overlays | Only dev cluster exists; structure repo for easy addition later |
    85→| Ingress NGINX | EOL March 2026; use NodePort for kind local access |
    86→| Azure Blob Storage in K8s | Azurite emulator not suitable for K8s; use placeholder images |

1→# Phase 26: ArgoCD GitOps - Research
     2→
     3→**Researched:** 2026-03-02
     4→**Domain:** ArgoCD GitOps, app-of-apps pattern, Kubernetes declarative management
     5→**Confidence:** HIGH
     6→
     7→## Summary
     8→
     9→Phase 26 installs ArgoCD v3.3.2 in the existing kind cluster and configures it to manage all MicroCommerce cluster resources from Git using the app-of-apps pattern. Currently, the bootstrap script (`infra/k8s/bootstrap.sh`) deploys all resources via manual `kubectl apply -k`. After this phase, ArgoCD watches the Git repository and automatically syncs the cluster state to match. Manual `kubectl apply` is replaced by ArgoCD's continuous reconciliation loop.
    10→
    11→The existing Kustomize structure (`infra/k8s/base/` + `infra/k8s/overlays/dev/`) is already well-suited for ArgoCD. ArgoCD natively detects and renders Kustomize directories when a `kustomization.yaml` is present. The app-of-apps pattern creates a root Application that points to a directory of child Application manifests, where each child Application points to a specific Kustomize path (e.g., `infra/k8s/overlays/dev/` or individual service directories). Self-heal mode ensures that if someone manually deletes a Deployment, ArgoCD automatically restores it within its 5-second default reconciliation cycle.
    12→
    13→The critical decision -- already locked in STATE.md -- is to use ArgoCD v3.3.2 specifically. Versions 3.3.0 and 3.3.1 had a client-side apply migration bug (GitHub issue #26279) that caused `failed to perform client-side apply migration` errors. This was fixed in v3.3.2 (released 2026-02-22).
    14→
    15→**Primary recommendation:** Install ArgoCD v3.3.2 in the `argocd` namespace with the non-HA `install.yaml` manifest, expose the UI via NodePort on port 30443 (mapped to host 38443), create a root app-of-apps Application that references a new `infra/k8s/argocd/apps/` directory containing one child Application YAML per service, each pointing to the appropriate Kustomize path.
    16→
    17→<phase_requirements>
    18→## Phase Requirements
    19→
    20→| ID | Description | Research Support |
    21→|----|-------------|-----------------|
    22→| GOPS-01 | ArgoCD is installed in the kind cluster and manages all services | ArgoCD v3.3.2 non-HA `install.yaml` installed via `kubectl apply --server-side --force-conflicts` in the `argocd` namespace. Self-heal + auto-sync enabled on all Applications. NodePort service exposes UI on port 30443 (host 38443). |
    23→| GOPS-02 | App-of-apps root Application manages per-service child Applications | Root Application in `infra/k8s/argocd/root-app.yaml` points to `infra/k8s/argocd/apps/` directory. Each child Application YAML defines one service (postgres, rabbitmq, keycloak, apiservice, gateway, web) pointing to its Kustomize path with auto-sync and self-heal enabled. |
    24→</phase_requirements>
    25→
    26→## Standard Stack
    27→
    28→### Core
    29→| Tool | Version | Purpose | Why Standard |
    30→|------|---------|---------|--------------|
    31→| ArgoCD | v3.3.2 | GitOps continuous delivery for Kubernetes | De facto standard K8s GitOps tool; CNCF graduated project; native Kustomize support; v3.3.2 specifically chosen per project decision to avoid client-side apply migration bug in v3.3.0/3.3.1 |
    32→| Kustomize | built-in (kubectl) | Manifest customization | Already used in Phases 24-25; ArgoCD auto-detects kustomization.yaml files |
    33→
    34→### Supporting
    35→| Tool | Version | Purpose | When to Use |
    36→|------|---------|---------|-------------|
    37→| kubectl | existing | Apply ArgoCD install manifest, port-forward, debugging | Initial ArgoCD installation before ArgoCD manages itself |
    38→| argocd CLI | v3.3.2 | Retrieve initial admin password, manual sync/debugging | Optional -- all operations can be done via `kubectl` and the UI |
    39→
    40→### Alternatives Considered
    41→| Instead of | Could Use | Tradeoff |
    42→|------------|-----------|----------|
    43→| App-of-apps | ApplicationSets | ApplicationSets are more dynamic (generators), but app-of-apps is simpler for a fixed set of 6 services; the project REQUIREMENTS.md explicitly requires "App-of-apps root Application" |
    44→| ArgoCD non-HA install | ArgoCD HA install | HA install runs 3+ replicas of each component -- overkill for a single-node kind cluster |
    45→| Plain YAML child apps | Helm-templated child apps | Plain YAML is simpler and sufficient; no templating needed for 6 static child apps |
    46→
    47→**Installation:**
    48→```bash
    49→kubectl create namespace argocd
    50→kubectl apply -n argocd --server-side --force-conflicts \
    51→  -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/argoproj/argo-cd/v3.3.2/manifests/install.yaml
    52→```
    53→
    54→## Architecture Patterns
    55→
    56→### Recommended Directory Structure
    57→```
    58→infra/k8s/
    59→  argocd/
    60→    root-app.yaml             # Root app-of-apps Application (applied manually once)
    61→    apps/                     # Directory of child Application manifests
    62→      postgres.yaml           # Points to infra/k8s/base/postgres/
    63→      rabbitmq.yaml           # Points to infra/k8s/base/rabbitmq/
    64→      keycloak.yaml           # Points to infra/k8s/base/keycloak/
    65→      apiservice.yaml         # Points to infra/k8s/overlays/dev/ (or base/apiservice/)
    66→      gateway.yaml            # Points to infra/k8s/overlays/dev/ (or base/gateway/)
    67→      web.yaml                # Points to infra/k8s/overlays/dev/ (or base/web/)
    68→  base/                       # (existing) Environment-neutral manifests
    69→    kustomization.yaml        # Root kustomization referencing all sub-dirs
    70→    namespace.yaml
    71→    postgres/
    72→    rabbitmq/
    73→    keycloak/
    74→    apiservice/
    75→    gateway/
    76→    web/
    77→  overlays/
    78→    dev/
    79→      kustomization.yaml      # (existing) Patches image tags for dev
    80→```
    81→
    82→### Pattern 1: App-of-Apps with Plain YAML Children
    83→**What:** A root Application whose source path contains plain YAML Application manifests. ArgoCD recursively creates child Applications from this directory.
    84→**When to use:** Fixed set of services that rarely change in number. Simpler than ApplicationSets for small, static service lists.
    85→**Key design decision:** Each child Application should point to the **Kustomize overlay path** (`infra/k8s/overlays/dev/`) rather than individual base directories, because the overlay is where image tags are patched. However, since the current overlay aggregates ALL services via `resources: [../../base]`, each child cannot independently point to the overlay without syncing everything. The solution is to restructure: either (a) have one Application point to the full overlay, or (b) split the overlay into per-service overlays.
    86→
    87→**Recommended approach (a) -- single dev overlay Application + per-infra base Applications:**
    88→- Infrastructure services (postgres, rabbitmq, keycloak) are managed as individual child Applications pointing to their base directories (they have no overlay patches)
    89→- Application services (apiservice, gateway, web) are managed by a single child Application pointing to `infra/k8s/overlays/dev/` which includes the full stack
    90→
    91→**Alternative approach -- more granular:** Restructure overlays to per-service directories. This is cleaner for Phase 27 (CI image tag updates per service) but requires refactoring the existing overlay. Can be done now or deferred.
    92→
    93→**Example root Application:**
    94→```yaml
    95→# Source: https://argo-cd.readthedocs.io/en/stable/operator-manual/cluster-bootstrapping/
    96→apiVersion: argoproj.io/v1alpha1
    97→kind: Application
    98→metadata:
    99→  name: micro-commerce-root
   100→  namespace: argocd
   101→  finalizers:
   102→  - resources-finalizer.argocd.argoproj.io
   103→spec:
   104→  project: default
   105→  source:
   106→    repoURL: https://github.com/baotoq/micro-commerce.git
   107→    targetRevision: master
   108→    path: infra/k8s/argocd/apps
   109→  destination:
   110→    server: https://kubernetes.default.svc
   111→    namespace: argocd
   112→  syncPolicy:
   113→    automated:
   114→      selfHeal: true
   115→      prune: true
   116→```
   117→
   118→**Example child Application (infrastructure service):**
   119→```yaml
   120→# Source: https://argo-cd.readthedocs.io/en/stable/operator-manual/declarative-setup/
   121→apiVersion: argoproj.io/v1alpha1
   122→kind: Application
   123→metadata:
   124→  name: postgres
   125→  namespace: argocd
   126→  finalizers:
   127→  - resources-finalizer.argocd.argoproj.io
   128→spec:
   129→  project: default
   130→  source:
   131→    repoURL: https://github.com/baotoq/micro-commerce.git
   132→    targetRevision: master
   133→    path: infra/k8s/base/postgres
   134→  destination:
   135→    server: https://kubernetes.default.svc
   136→    namespace: micro-commerce
   137→  syncPolicy:
   138→    automated:
   139→      selfHeal: true
   140→      prune: true
   141→    syncOptions:
   142→    - CreateNamespace=true
   143→```
   144→
   145→**Example child Application (full dev overlay):**
   146→```yaml
   147→apiVersion: argoproj.io/v1alpha1
   148→kind: Application
   149→metadata:
   150→  name: micro-commerce-apps
   151→  namespace: argocd
   152→  finalizers:
   153→  - resources-finalizer.argocd.argoproj.io
   154→spec:
   155→  project: default
   156→  source:
   157→    repoURL: https://github.com/baotoq/micro-commerce.git
   158→    targetRevision: master
   159→    path: infra/k8s/overlays/dev
   160→  destination:
   161→    server: https://kubernetes.default.svc
   162→    namespace: micro-commerce
   163→  syncPolicy:
   164→    automated:
   165→      selfHeal: true
   166→      prune: true
   167→    syncOptions:
   168→    - CreateNamespace=true
   169→```
   170→
   171→### Pattern 2: ArgoCD NodePort Access for kind
   172→**What:** Patch the `argocd-server` Service to NodePort and add a kind extraPortMapping so the ArgoCD UI is reachable from the host.
   173→**When to use:** kind clusters where LoadBalancer is not available.
   174→**Example:**
   175→```yaml
   176→# NodePort service for argocd-server
   177→apiVersion: v1
   178→kind: Service
   179→metadata:
   180→  name: argocd-server-nodeport
   181→  namespace: argocd
   182→spec:
   183→  type: NodePort
   184→  selector:
   185→    app.kubernetes.io/name: argocd-server
   186→  ports:
   187→  - name: https
   188→    port: 443
   189→    targetPort: 8080
   190→    nodePort: 30443
   191→```
   192→
   193→And in `kind-config.yaml`, add the port mapping:
   194→```yaml
   195→- containerPort: 30443
   196→  hostPort: 38443
   197→  protocol: TCP
   198→```
   199→
   200→### Pattern 3: Disable TLS for Local Dev
   201→**What:** ArgoCD server runs HTTPS by default. For kind local dev, disable TLS so NodePort works without certificate issues.
   202→**When to use:** Local development clusters only.
   203→**Example:**
   204→```yaml
   205→# Patch argocd-cmd-params-cm ConfigMap
   206→apiVersion: v1
   207→kind: ConfigMap
   208→metadata:
   209→  name: argocd-cmd-params-cm
   210→  namespace: argocd
   211→data:
   212→  server.insecure: "true"
   213→```
   214→After patching, restart the argocd-server deployment:
   215→```bash
   216→kubectl rollout restart deployment argocd-server -n argocd
   217→```
   218→
   219→### Anti-Patterns to Avoid
   220→- **Deeply nested app-of-apps:** Do not create apps that create apps that create apps. One level of nesting (root -> children) is sufficient.
   221→- **Using HEAD targetRevision in production:** Use a specific branch name (`master`) or commit SHA. HEAD can cause unexpected syncs.
   222→- **Mixing directory and Kustomize sources:** If a path contains `kustomization.yaml`, ArgoCD uses Kustomize mode. If you set `directory.recurse: true` on a path that also has `kustomization.yaml`, it will fail. Do not mix modes.
   223→- **Omitting finalizers:** Without `resources-finalizer.argocd.argoproj.io`, deleting an Application does not delete its managed resources, leading to orphaned resources.
   224→- **SealedSecrets conflict:** ArgoCD will try to sync SealedSecret resources. Since SealedSecrets are dynamically generated by the bootstrap script and may not exist in Git (they are `.gitignore`d or generated per-cluster), ArgoCD may report them as OutOfSync. Plan for this.
   225→
   226→## Don't Hand-Roll
   227→
   228→| Problem | Don't Build | Use Instead | Why |
   229→|---------|-------------|-------------|-----|
   230→| GitOps sync controller | Custom kubectl-apply-on-commit script | ArgoCD automated sync + self-heal | Handles drift detection, health checks, retry logic, UI, RBAC, multi-app orchestration |
   231→| App dependency ordering | Custom init containers or scripts for service startup order | ArgoCD sync waves + health checks | ArgoCD waits for resources to be Healthy before proceeding to next wave |
   232→| Git webhook reconciliation | Custom GitHub webhook -> kubectl pipeline | ArgoCD's built-in Git polling (3 min default) or webhook receiver | ArgoCD has a built-in webhook server that triggers immediate sync on push |
   233→| Resource health dashboard | Custom monitoring of pod status | ArgoCD UI resource tree view | Shows real-time health for every resource in every application |
   234→
   235→**Key insight:** ArgoCD replaces the manual `kubectl apply -k` commands in the bootstrap script for application deployment. The bootstrap script still handles one-time infrastructure setup (kind cluster creation, SealedSecrets controller installation, secret sealing), but ArgoCD takes over the ongoing management of all Kubernetes resources from Git.
   236→
   237→## Common Pitfalls
   238→
   239→### Pitfall 1: SealedSecrets Not in Git
   240→**What goes wrong:** ArgoCD reports applications as OutOfSync because `sealed-secret.yaml` files are generated dynamically by the bootstrap script and may not be committed to Git (they contain cluster-specific encrypted data).
   241→**Why it happens:** SealedSecrets are encrypted with the controller's public key, which is unique per cluster. The sealed YAML files in the repo are valid only for the cluster that sealed them.
   242→**How to avoid:** Commit the `sealed-secret.yaml` files to Git after the bootstrap script generates them. They are safe to commit (encrypted). The bootstrap script already generates them to `base/{service}/sealed-secret.yaml` -- ensure they are committed and referenced in the service's `kustomization.yaml`.
   243→**Warning signs:** Applications stuck in OutOfSync; pods failing with `secret not found` errors.
   244→
   245→### Pitfall 2: Namespace Ownership Conflict
   246→**What goes wrong:** Multiple ArgoCD Applications try to manage the same `micro-commerce` namespace, causing sync conflicts.
   247→**Why it happens:** The `namespace.yaml` is referenced in `base/kustomization.yaml`, and if both the root kustomization and individual service applications try to create the namespace, ArgoCD reports conflicts.
   248→**How to avoid:** Have exactly one Application own the namespace resource, or use `syncOptions: [CreateNamespace=true]` on child apps and remove the explicit `namespace.yaml` from Kustomize resources. Alternatively, manage the namespace outside ArgoCD (in the bootstrap script).
   249→**Warning signs:** `ComparisonError` or `already exists` errors in ArgoCD sync status.
   250→
   251→### Pitfall 3: Client-Side Apply Migration Bug (v3.3.0/v3.3.1)
   252→**What goes wrong:** ArgoCD fails to sync with `failed to perform client-side apply migration` errors.
   253→**Why it happens:** A regression in ArgoCD v3.3.0 and v3.3.1 broke the client-side apply migration path (GitHub #26279).
   254→**How to avoid:** Use v3.3.2 specifically, which contains the fix.
   255→**Warning signs:** Sync errors mentioning "client-side apply migration" immediately after ArgoCD installation.
   256→
   257→### Pitfall 4: ArgoCD CRD Size Requires Server-Side Apply
   258→**What goes wrong:** `kubectl apply` fails with "metadata.annotations too long" or similar error.
   259→**Why it happens:** ArgoCD CRDs exceed the 256KB annotation size limit for client-side apply.
   260→**How to avoid:** Always use `--server-side --force-conflicts` flags when installing ArgoCD manifests.
   261→**Warning signs:** kubectl errors during initial ArgoCD installation mentioning annotation size.
   262→
   263→### Pitfall 5: Image Pull Failures for Local kind Images
   264→**What goes wrong:** ArgoCD syncs the manifests but pods fail with `ImagePullBackOff` because the locally-built images (e.g., `apiservice:dev`) are not available in kind.
   265→**Why it happens:** ArgoCD syncing manifests does not load Docker images into kind. Images must be pre-loaded via `kind load docker-image`.
   266→**How to avoid:** The bootstrap script must continue to handle image building and loading into kind before ArgoCD takes over sync. ArgoCD manages the manifest state, not the image availability.
   267→**Warning signs:** Pods stuck in `ImagePullBackOff` or `ErrImagePull` after ArgoCD sync.
   268→
   269→### Pitfall 6: Kustomize Image Transformer Not Applied by Individual Base Apps
   270→**What goes wrong:** Child Applications pointing to individual `base/{service}/` directories get the base image tags (e.g., `apiservice:dev` hardcoded in deployment.yaml) rather than the overlay-patched tags.
   271→**Why it happens:** The Kustomize `images:` transformer only applies when rendering through the overlay path that contains it.
   272→**How to avoid:** Either point app services to the overlay (not individual base dirs), or ensure the base deployment.yaml already has the correct image tags.
   273→**Warning signs:** Pods running with wrong image tags; overlay image patches not taking effect.
   274→
   275→## Code Examples
   276→
   277→Verified patterns from official ArgoCD documentation:
   278→
   279→### ArgoCD Installation in Bootstrap Script
   280→```bash
   281→# Source: https://argo-cd.readthedocs.io/en/stable/getting_started/
   282→# Install ArgoCD v3.3.2 (non-HA, single cluster)
   283→kubectl create namespace argocd
   284→kubectl apply -n argocd --server-side --force-conflicts \
   285→  -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/argoproj/argo-cd/v3.3.2/manifests/install.yaml
   286→
   287→# Wait for ArgoCD to be ready
   288→kubectl rollout status deployment argocd-server -n argocd --timeout=120s
   289→kubectl rollout status deployment argocd-repo-server -n argocd --timeout=120s
   290→kubectl rollout status deployment argocd-applicationset-controller -n argocd --timeout=120s
   291→
   292→# Disable TLS for local kind access (HTTP on port 8080)
   293→kubectl patch configmap argocd-cmd-params-cm -n argocd \
   294→  --type merge -p '{"data":{"server.insecure":"true"}}'
   295→kubectl rollout restart deployment argocd-server -n argocd
   296→kubectl rollout status deployment argocd-server -n argocd --timeout=120s
   297→
   298→# Expose via NodePort
   299→kubectl apply -f "$SCRIPT_DIR/argocd/argocd-server-nodeport.yaml"
   300→
   301→# Retrieve initial admin password
   302→ARGOCD_PASSWORD=$(kubectl -n argocd get secret argocd-initial-admin-secret \
   303→  -o jsonpath='{.data.password}' | base64 --decode)
   304→echo "ArgoCD UI: http://localhost:38443 (user: admin, pass: $ARGOCD_PASSWORD)"
   305→```

   372→## State of the Art
   373→
   374→| Old Approach | Current Approach | When Changed | Impact |
   375→|--------------|------------------|--------------|--------|
   376→| `kubectl apply -k` in scripts | ArgoCD automated sync from Git | Ongoing (this phase) | Drift detection, auto-heal, audit trail, UI visibility |
   377→| ArgoCD v2.x with separate CRD install | ArgoCD v3.x with `--server-side --force-conflicts` | ArgoCD 3.0 (2025) | CRDs bundled in install.yaml; must use server-side apply due to size |
   378→| Client-side apply as default | Server-side apply migration in v3.3.x | ArgoCD 3.3.0 | v3.3.0/3.3.1 had a migration bug; v3.3.2 fixes it |
   379→| App-of-apps as primary pattern | ApplicationSets as recommended for dynamic workloads | ArgoCD 2.6+ | For static service lists like this project, app-of-apps remains simpler and more explicit |
   380→
   381→**Deprecated/outdated:**
   382→- ArgoCD v2.x: Still supported but v3.x is current stable line
   383→- `argocd-util` CLI: Replaced by `argocd admin` subcommands
   384→- Client-side apply default: v3.3+ migrates to server-side apply
   385→
   386→## Open Questions
   387→
   388→1. **App granularity: one child per service vs one child for the full overlay?**
   389→   - What we know: The current Kustomize overlay (`overlays/dev/`) aggregates all services in one kustomization. Individual base directories exist per service. ArgoCD can point to either.
   390→   - What's unclear: Whether to create 6 individual child Applications (one per service) each pointing to its base directory, or fewer Applications pointing to the overlay. Per-service apps are more granular but the image tag overlay only applies when rendered through `overlays/dev/`.
   391→   - Recommendation: Use a hybrid approach -- one child Application for the full `overlays/dev/` Kustomize path (covers all 6 services with correct image tags), plus the namespace managed by `CreateNamespace=true` sync option. This gives the app-of-apps structure while respecting the existing Kustomize overlay architecture. If true per-service granularity is needed for Phase 27 CI, refactor overlays then.
   392→
   393→2. **SealedSecret YAML files in Git**
   394→   - What we know: The bootstrap script generates `sealed-secret.yaml` files dynamically. They are cluster-specific (encrypted with that cluster's controller key).
   395→   - What's unclear: Whether these files are currently committed to Git or `.gitignore`d. ArgoCD needs them in Git to sync.
   396→   - Recommendation: Verify during implementation. If not committed, the bootstrap script should commit them after generation, or ArgoCD should be configured to ignore missing SealedSecret resources.
   397→
   398→3. **Bootstrap script modification scope**
   399→   - What we know: The bootstrap script currently does everything: cluster creation, SealedSecrets, manifests, image building.
   400→   - What's unclear: How much of the script should change. ArgoCD replaces `kubectl apply -k` but not cluster/image/secret operations.
   401→   - Recommendation: Keep the bootstrap script for: kind cluster creation, SealedSecrets controller install, secret sealing, image building/loading, and ArgoCD installation + root app creation. Remove the manual `kubectl apply -k` steps that ArgoCD now handles.
   402→
   403→## Validation Architecture
   404→
   405→### Test Framework
   406→| Property | Value |
   407→|----------|-------|
   408→| Framework | Manual validation via kubectl + ArgoCD UI (infrastructure phase, no unit test framework) |
   409→| Config file | none -- validation is via kubectl commands |
   410→| Quick run command | `kubectl get applications -n argocd` |
   411→| Full suite command | `kubectl get applications -n argocd -o json \| jq '.items[] \| {name: .metadata.name, health: .status.health.status, sync: .status.sync.status}'` |
   412→
   413→### Phase Requirements to Test Map
   414→| Req ID | Behavior | Test Type | Automated Command | File Exists? |
   415→|--------|----------|-----------|-------------------|-------------|
   416→| GOPS-01 | ArgoCD installed, all services Synced and Healthy | smoke | `kubectl get applications -n argocd -o jsonpath='{range .items[*]}{.metadata.name}{"\t"}{.status.sync.status}{"\t"}{.status.health.status}{"\n"}{end}'` | N/A -- kubectl |
   417→| GOPS-01 | ArgoCD UI reachable | manual-only | Open `http://localhost:38443` in browser | N/A -- manual |
   418→| GOPS-02 | App-of-apps root manages children | smoke | `kubectl get applications -n argocd -l app.kubernetes.io/instance=micro-commerce-root` | N/A -- kubectl |
   419→| GOPS-02 | Self-heal restores deleted deployment | smoke | `kubectl delete deployment apiservice -n micro-commerce && sleep 10 && kubectl get deployment apiservice -n micro-commerce` | N/A -- kubectl |
   420→
   421→### Sampling Rate
   422→- **Per task commit:** `kubectl get applications -n argocd` (verify ArgoCD sees apps)
   423→- **Per wave merge:** Full validation: all apps Synced+Healthy, delete+restore test
   424→- **Phase gate:** All three success criteria verified before `/gsd:verify-work`
   425→
   426→### Wave 0 Gaps
   427→None -- this phase creates Kubernetes manifests and shell scripts, not application code with test frameworks.
   428→
   429→## Sources
   430→
   431→### Primary (HIGH confidence)
   432→- [ArgoCD v3.3.2 Release](https://github.com/argoproj/argo-cd/releases/tag/v3.3.2) - Confirmed client-side apply migration bug fix, release date 2026-02-22
   433→- [ArgoCD Getting Started](https://argo-cd.readthedocs.io/en/stable/getting_started/) - Installation commands, admin password retrieval, server-side apply requirements
   434→- [ArgoCD Cluster Bootstrapping / App-of-Apps](https://argo-cd.readthedocs.io/en/stable/operator-manual/cluster-bootstrapping/) - Root Application pattern, directory structure, child Application examples, finalizer requirement
   435→- [ArgoCD Declarative Setup](https://argo-cd.readthedocs.io/en/stable/operator-manual/declarative-setup/) - Application CRD spec, AppProject config, repository secrets
   436→- [ArgoCD Kustomize Support](https://argo-cd.readthedocs.io/en/stable/user-guide/kustomize/) - Auto-detection of kustomization.yaml, overlay path configuration
   437→- [ArgoCD Auto Sync](https://argo-cd.readthedocs.io/en/stable/user-guide/auto_sync/) - selfHeal, prune, automated sync YAML spec
   438→- [ArgoCD Directory Source](https://argo-cd.readthedocs.io/en/latest/user-guide/directory/) - Plain YAML directory handling, recurse option, include/exclude patterns
   439→- [ArgoCD Installation](https://argo-cd.readthedocs.io/en/stable/operator-manual/installation/) - install.yaml vs namespace-install.yaml, HA vs non-HA
   440→- [ArgoCD TLS Configuration](https://argo-cd.readthedocs.io/en/stable/operator-manual/tls/) - Disabling TLS via argocd-cmd-params-cm ConfigMap
   441→
   442→### Secondary (MEDIUM confidence)
   443→- [ArgoCD Resource Health](https://argo-cd.readthedocs.io/en/latest/operator-manual/health/) - Built-in health checks for Deployments, StatefulSets, Services
   444→- [CNCF Blog: App-of-Apps Pattern](https://www.cncf.io/blog/2025/10/07/managing-kubernetes-workloads-using-the-app-of-apps-pattern-in-argocd-2/) - Best practices, security considerations
   445→
   446→### Tertiary (LOW confidence)
   447→- None -- all findings verified with official documentation
   448→
   449→## Metadata
   450→
   451→**Confidence breakdown:**
   452→- Standard stack: HIGH - ArgoCD v3.3.2 confirmed via official GitHub releases; installation method from official docs
   453→- Architecture: HIGH - App-of-apps pattern documented in official ArgoCD cluster bootstrapping guide; Kustomize integration confirmed via official docs
   454→- Pitfalls: HIGH - Client-side apply bug confirmed in v3.3.2 release notes; SealedSecrets interaction based on understanding of both tools; namespace conflict well-documented in ArgoCD community

1→kind: Cluster
     2→apiVersion: kind.x-k8s.io/v1alpha4
     3→name: micro-commerce
     4→nodes:
     5→- role: control-plane
     6→  extraPortMappings:
     7→  # PostgreSQL (NodePort 30432 -> host 35432)
     8→  - containerPort: 30432
     9→    hostPort: 35432
    10→    protocol: TCP
    11→  # RabbitMQ Management UI (NodePort 30672 -> host 35672)
    12→  - containerPort: 30672
    13→    hostPort: 35672
    14→    protocol: TCP
    15→  # Keycloak (NodePort 30080 -> host 38080)
    16→  - containerPort: 30080
    17→    hostPort: 38080
    18→    protocol: TCP
    19→  # Gateway (NodePort 30800 -> host 38800)
    20→  - containerPort: 30800
    21→    hostPort: 38800
    22→    protocol: TCP
    23→

1→#!/usr/bin/env bash
     2→set -euo pipefail
     3→
     4→SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
     5→PROJECT_ROOT="$(cd "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)"
     6→CLUSTER_NAME="micro-commerce"
     7→NAMESPACE="micro-commerce"
     8→SEALED_SECRETS_VERSION="v0.27.3"
     9→
    10→# Color output
    11→GREEN='\033[0;32m'
    12→YELLOW='\033[1;33m'
    13→NC='\033[0m' # No Color
    14→
    15→info() { echo -e "${GREEN}[INFO]${NC} $*"; }
    16→warn() { echo -e "${YELLOW}[WARN]${NC} $*"; }
    17→
    18→# --- Step 1: Create kind cluster ---
    19→if kind get clusters 2>/dev/null | grep -q "^${CLUSTER_NAME}$"; then
    20→  info "Cluster '${CLUSTER_NAME}' already exists, skipping creation."
    21→else
    22→  info "Creating kind cluster '${CLUSTER_NAME}'..."
    23→  kind create cluster --name "$CLUSTER_NAME" --config "$SCRIPT_DIR/kind-config.yaml"
    24→fi
    25→
    26→# --- Step 2: Set kubectl context ---
    27→info "Setting kubectl context..."
    28→kubectl cluster-info --context "kind-${CLUSTER_NAME}" >/dev/null 2>&1
    29→kubectl config use-context "kind-${CLUSTER_NAME}"
    30→
    31→# --- Step 3: Install SealedSecrets controller ---
    32→info "Installing SealedSecrets controller ${SEALED_SECRETS_VERSION}..."
    33→kubectl apply -f "https://github.com/bitnami-labs/sealed-secrets/releases/download/${SEALED_SECRETS_VERSION}/controller.yaml"
    34→
    35→# --- Step 4: Wait for SealedSecrets controller ---
    36→info "Waiting for SealedSecrets controller to be ready..."
    37→kubectl rollout status deployment/sealed-secrets-controller -n kube-system --timeout=120s
    38→
    39→# --- Step 5: Create namespace ---
    40→info "Creating namespace '${NAMESPACE}'..."
    41→kubectl create namespace "$NAMESPACE" --dry-run=client -o yaml | kubectl apply -f -
    42→
    43→# --- Step 6: Seal dev secrets ---
    44→info "Sealing dev secrets..."
    45→
    46→seal_secret() {
    47→  local name="$1"
    48→  local output="$2"
    49→  shift 2
    50→  # Remaining args are --from-literal pairs
    51→
    52→  kubectl create secret generic "$name" \
    53→    --namespace "$NAMESPACE" \
    54→    "$@" \
    55→    --dry-run=client -o yaml | \
    56→  kubeseal \
    57→    --controller-name=sealed-secrets-controller \
    58→    --controller-namespace=kube-system \
    59→    --format yaml > "$output"
    60→
    61→  info "  Sealed: $name -> $output"
    62→}
    63→
    64→seal_secret "postgres-credentials" \
    65→  "$SCRIPT_DIR/base/postgres/sealed-secret.yaml" \
    66→  --from-literal=username=postgres \
    67→  --from-literal=password=postgres
    68→
    69→seal_secret "rabbitmq-credentials" \
    70→  "$SCRIPT_DIR/base/rabbitmq/sealed-secret.yaml" \
    71→  --from-literal=username=guest \
    72→  --from-literal=password=guest
    73→
    74→seal_secret "keycloak-credentials" \
    75→  "$SCRIPT_DIR/base/keycloak/sealed-secret.yaml" \
    76→  --from-literal=admin-username=admin \
    77→  --from-literal=admin-password=admin
    78→
    79→# --- Step 7: Apply infrastructure manifests ---
    80→info "Applying infrastructure manifests..."
    81→kubectl apply -k "$SCRIPT_DIR/base/"
    82→
    83→# --- Step 8: Wait for pods ---
    84→info "Waiting for PostgreSQL..."
    85→kubectl wait --for=condition=ready pod -l app=postgres -n "$NAMESPACE" --timeout=120s
    86→
    87→info "Waiting for RabbitMQ..."
    88→kubectl wait --for=condition=ready pod -l app=rabbitmq -n "$NAMESPACE" --timeout=120s
    89→
    90→info "Waiting for Keycloak (may take up to 3 minutes for realm import)..."
    91→kubectl wait --for=condition=ready pod -l app=keycloak -n "$NAMESPACE" --timeout=180s
    92→
    93→# --- Step 9: Build application container images ---
    94→info "Building application container images..."
    95→
    96→info "  Building ApiService..."
    97→dotnet publish "$PROJECT_ROOT/src/MicroCommerce.ApiService/MicroCommerce.ApiService.csproj" \
    98→  /t:PublishContainer \
    99→  -p:ContainerRegistry="" \
   100→  -p:ContainerRepository=apiservice \
   101→  -p:ContainerImageTag=dev \
   102→  --nologo -v quiet
   103→
   104→info "  Building Gateway..."
   105→dotnet publish "$PROJECT_ROOT/src/MicroCommerce.Gateway/MicroCommerce.Gateway.csproj" \
   106→  /t:PublishContainer \
   107→  -p:ContainerRegistry="" \
   108→  -p:ContainerRepository=gateway \
   109→  -p:ContainerImageTag=dev \
   110→  --nologo -v quiet
   111→
   112→info "  Building Web..."
   113→docker build \
   114→  -f "$PROJECT_ROOT/src/MicroCommerce.Web/Dockerfile" \
   115→  -t web:dev \
   116→  "$PROJECT_ROOT/src/MicroCommerce.Web" \
   117→  --quiet
   118→
   119→# --- Step 10: Load images into kind ---
   120→info "Loading images into kind cluster..."
   121→kind load docker-image apiservice:dev --name "$CLUSTER_NAME"
   122→kind load docker-image gateway:dev --name "$CLUSTER_NAME"
   123→kind load docker-image web:dev --name "$CLUSTER_NAME"
   124→
   125→# --- Step 11: Apply application manifests ---
   126→info "Applying application manifests..."
   127→kubectl apply -k "$SCRIPT_DIR/overlays/dev/"
   128→
   129→# --- Step 12: Wait for application pods ---
   130→info "Waiting for ApiService (may take up to 3 minutes for first-boot migrations)..."
   131→kubectl wait --for=condition=ready pod -l app=apiservice -n "$NAMESPACE" --timeout=180s
   132→
   133→info "Waiting for Gateway..."
   134→kubectl wait --for=condition=ready pod -l app=gateway -n "$NAMESPACE" --timeout=120s
   135→
   136→info "Waiting for Web..."
   137→kubectl wait --for=condition=ready pod -l app=web -n "$NAMESPACE" --timeout=120s
   138→
   139→# --- Step 13: Print access info ---
   140→echo ""
   141→info "Full stack ready!"
   142→echo ""
   143→echo "Access points:"
   144→echo "  PostgreSQL:          localhost:35432 (user: postgres, pass: postgres, db: appdb)"
   145→echo "  RabbitMQ Management: http://localhost:35672 (user: guest, pass: guest)"
   146→echo "  Keycloak Admin:      http://localhost:38080 (user: admin, pass: admin)"
   147→echo "  Gateway (API):       http://localhost:38800"
   148→echo ""
   149→echo "Storefront: http://localhost:38800 (via Gateway)"
   150→echo ""
   151→echo "Useful commands:"
   152→echo "  kubectl get pods -n ${NAMESPACE}"
   153→echo "  kubectl logs -f deployment/apiservice -n ${NAMESPACE}"
   154→echo "  kubectl logs -f deployment/gateway -n ${NAMESPACE}"
   155→echo "  kubectl logs -f deployment/web -n ${NAMESPACE}"
   156→echo "  kind delete cluster --name ${CLUSTER_NAME}"
   157→

<task type="auto">
  <name>Task 1: Update bootstrap.sh to install ArgoCD and replace manual kubectl apply with GitOps</name>
  <files>infra/k8s/bootstrap.sh</files>
  <action>
    Rewrite the bootstrap script to integrate ArgoCD installation and hand off resource management. The new flow:

    **Steps 1-2: KEEP UNCHANGED** (kind cluster creation, kubectl context)

    **Steps 3-4: KEEP UNCHANGED** (SealedSecrets controller install + wait)

    **Step 5: KEEP UNCHANGED** (create namespace)

    **Step 6: Seal dev secrets -- MODIFY the seal_secret calls.** Keep the seal_secret function and all 3 seal_secret calls (postgres, rabbitmq, keycloak) exactly as-is. After all 3 secrets are sealed, add explicit kubectl apply for each sealed-secret.yaml file:
    ```bash
    info "Applying sealed secrets..."
    kubectl apply -f "$SCRIPT_DIR/base/postgres/sealed-secret.yaml"
    kubectl apply -f "$SCRIPT_DIR/base/rabbitmq/sealed-secret.yaml"
    kubectl apply -f "$SCRIPT_DIR/base/keycloak/sealed-secret.yaml"
    ```
    WHY: sealed-secret.yaml is no longer referenced in base kustomization.yaml files (removed in Plan 01), so they must be applied explicitly. These must exist in the cluster before ArgoCD syncs the Deployments that reference the Secrets.

    **Step 7: REMOVE** the old `kubectl apply -k "$SCRIPT_DIR/base/"` line. ArgoCD will handle this now.

    **Step 8: REMOVE** the old infra pod wait commands. ArgoCD sync handles deployment.

    **NEW Step 7: Install ArgoCD v3.3.2**
    ```bash
    # --- Step 7: Install ArgoCD ---
    ARGOCD_VERSION="v3.3.2"
    info "Installing ArgoCD ${ARGOCD_VERSION}..."
    kubectl create namespace argocd --dry-run=client -o yaml | kubectl apply -f -
    kubectl apply -n argocd --server-side --force-conflicts \
      -f "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/argoproj/argo-cd/${ARGOCD_VERSION}/manifests/install.yaml"
    ```
    Use `--server-side --force-conflicts` because ArgoCD CRDs exceed 256KB annotation limit for client-side apply. Use idempotent namespace creation same as Step 5 pattern.

    **NEW Step 8: Wait for ArgoCD to be ready**
    ```bash
    info "Waiting for ArgoCD to be ready..."
    kubectl rollout status deployment argocd-server -n argocd --timeout=120s
    kubectl rollout status deployment argocd-repo-server -n argocd --timeout=120s
    kubectl rollout status deployment argocd-applicationset-controller -n argocd --timeout=120s
    ```

    **NEW Step 9: Configure ArgoCD for local access**
    ```bash
    info "Configuring ArgoCD for local access..."
    kubectl apply -f "$SCRIPT_DIR/argocd/argocd-cmd-params-cm.yaml"
    kubectl rollout restart deployment argocd-server -n argocd
    kubectl rollout status deployment argocd-server -n argocd --timeout=120s
    kubectl apply -f "$SCRIPT_DIR/argocd/argocd-server-nodeport.yaml"
    ```
    Apply the insecure ConfigMap, restart argocd-server to pick it up, wait for it to be ready, then apply the NodePort service.

    **Steps 10-11: KEEP as-is** (build app images, load into kind). Renumber to Step 10 and Step 11.

    **NEW Step 12: Apply ArgoCD root application**
    ```bash
    info "Applying ArgoCD root app-of-apps..."
    kubectl apply -f "$SCRIPT_DIR/argocd/root-app.yaml"
    ```
    This triggers ArgoCD to discover and sync all child Applications, which in turn deploy all infrastructure and application manifests from Git.

    **NEW Step 13: Wait for ArgoCD to sync all applications**
    ```bash
    info "Waiting for ArgoCD to sync all applications..."
    # Wait for all child apps to be created by the root app
    sleep 10

    # Wait for infrastructure pods
    info "Waiting for PostgreSQL..."
    kubectl wait --for=condition=ready pod -l app=postgres -n "$NAMESPACE" --timeout=120s

    info "Waiting for RabbitMQ..."
    kubectl wait --for=condition=ready pod -l app=rabbitmq -n "$NAMESPACE" --timeout=120s

    info "Waiting for Keycloak (may take up to 3 minutes for realm import)..."
    kubectl wait --for=condition=ready pod -l app=keycloak -n "$NAMESPACE" --timeout=180s

    # Wait for application pods
    info "Waiting for ApiService (may take up to 3 minutes for first-boot migrations)..."
    kubectl wait --for=condition=ready pod -l app=apiservice -n "$NAMESPACE" --timeout=180s

    info "Waiting for Gateway..."
    kubectl wait --for=condition=ready pod -l app=gateway -n "$NAMESPACE" --timeout=120s

    info "Waiting for Web..."
    kubectl wait --for=condition=ready pod -l app=web -n "$NAMESPACE" --timeout=120s
    ```
    Keep pod wait commands because the user needs to know when everything is ready. ArgoCD sync triggers deployment, but we still wait for pods to be Running.

    **Step 14: Print access info -- UPDATE** to include ArgoCD:
    Add these lines to the access info section:
    ```bash
    ARGOCD_PASSWORD=$(kubectl -n argocd get secret argocd-initial-admin-secret \
      -o jsonpath='{.data.password}' | base64 --decode)

    # In the echo block, add:
    echo "  ArgoCD UI:           http://localhost:38443 (user: admin, pass: ${ARGOCD_PASSWORD})"
    ```
    Also add to Useful commands:
    ```bash
    echo "  kubectl get applications -n argocd"
    ```

    **Summary of step numbers in the new script:**
    1. Create kind cluster
    2. Set kubectl context
    3. Install SealedSecrets controller
    4. Wait for SealedSecrets controller
    5. Create namespace
    6. Seal dev secrets + apply sealed secrets directly
    7. Install ArgoCD v3.3.2
    8. Wait for ArgoCD
    9. Configure ArgoCD for local access (TLS disable + NodePort)
    10. Build application container images
    11. Load images into kind
    12. Apply ArgoCD root app-of-apps
    13. Wait for ArgoCD sync and all pods
    14. Print access info (including ArgoCD URL and password)
  </action>
  <verify>

## Milestones

- ✅ **v1.0 MVP** — Phases 1-10 (shipped 2026-02-13) — [archive](milestones/v1.0-ROADMAP.md)
- ✅ **v1.1 User Features** — Phases 11-14.3 (shipped 2026-02-14) — [archive](milestones/v1.1-ROADMAP.md)
- ✅ **v2.0 DDD Foundation** — Phases 15-22 (shipped 2026-02-25) — [archive](milestones/v2.0-ROADMAP.md)
- 🚧 **v3.0 Kubernetes & GitOps** — Phases 23-28 (in progress)

## Phases

<details>
<summary>✅ v1.0 MVP (Phases 1-10) — SHIPPED 2026-02-13</summary>

- [x] Phase 1: Foundation & Project Structure (6/6 plans) — completed 2026-01-30

</details>

- [x] Phase 14: Integration & Polish (3/3 plans) — completed 2026-02-14
- [x] Phase 14.1: Check DDD Approach Correctness (2/2 plans) — completed 2026-02-14
- [x] Phase 14.2: ValueObject Record Struct Migration (3/3 plans) — completed 2026-02-14

- [x] Phase 22: Wire Interceptors to DbContexts (1/1 plan) — completed 2026-02-25

</details>

---

### 🚧 v3.0 Kubernetes & GitOps (In Progress)

**Milestone Goal:** Deploy the full MicroCommerce stack to a kind-based Kubernetes cluster using GitOps via ArgoCD, with GitHub Actions CI building and pushing images to ghcr.io, Kustomize managing manifests, and Sealed Secrets handling credentials safely in Git.

- [x] **Phase 23: Dockerfiles and Container Image Pipeline** - Build production-ready images for all three services and push them to ghcr.io via GitHub Actions (completed 2026-02-25)
- [x] **Phase 24: Infrastructure Manifests and Secrets** - Deploy PostgreSQL, RabbitMQ, and Keycloak in the kind cluster with Sealed Secrets for all credentials (completed 2026-02-26)
- [x] **Phase 25: Application Manifests and MassTransit Transport** - Deploy ApiService, Gateway, and Web with Kustomize base/overlay structure and RabbitMQ transport support (completed 2026-02-26)
- [ ] **Phase 26: ArgoCD GitOps** - Install ArgoCD and wire app-of-apps to manage all cluster resources from Git
- [ ] **Phase 27: CI/CD GitOps Loop Closure** - Extend CI to commit SHA image tags back to the overlay, completing the full GitOps loop
- [ ] **Phase 28: Observability** - Deploy OTEL Collector and standalone Aspire Dashboard for in-cluster monitoring

## Phase Details

### Phase 23: Dockerfiles and Container Image Pipeline
**Goal**: Three production-ready container images exist in ghcr.io and are built automatically on every push to master
**Depends on**: Phase 22 (v2.0 complete)
**Requirements**: CONT-01, CONT-02, CONT-03, CONT-04, CICD-01
**Success Criteria** (what must be TRUE):
  1. `docker build` succeeds for ApiService, Gateway, and Web with no warnings or root-user violations
  2. `docker run` starts each service and the process responds to health checks within 30 seconds
  3. A push to master triggers the GitHub Actions workflow and all three images appear in ghcr.io tagged with the commit SHA
  4. Image sizes are reasonable: .NET images use chiseled runtime and Next.js uses standalone output with node:alpine
**Plans**: TBD

### Phase 24: Infrastructure Manifests and Secrets
**Goal**: PostgreSQL, RabbitMQ, and Keycloak run stably in the kind cluster with all credentials sealed in Git
**Depends on**: Phase 23
**Requirements**: INFRA-01, INFRA-02, INFRA-03, INFRA-04, INFRA-05, GOPS-03
**Success Criteria** (what must be TRUE):
  1. `kind create cluster --config kind-config.yaml` creates a cluster and all infrastructure pods reach Running state
  2. PostgreSQL data persists across pod restarts (PVC-backed StatefulSet)
  3. Keycloak admin UI is reachable and the MicroCommerce realm is pre-loaded from a ConfigMap
  4. No plaintext credentials exist in any committed YAML file; all secrets are SealedSecret objects
  5. Keycloak startup probe is configured with sufficient timeout (15s delay + 60s probing window) to survive slow first-boot realm import
**Plans**: 4 plans
- [x] 24-01-PLAN.md -- Kind cluster config, Kustomize scaffold, PostgreSQL StatefulSet
- [x] 24-02-PLAN.md -- RabbitMQ Deployment, Keycloak Deployment with realm import
- [x] 24-03-PLAN.md -- Bootstrap script with SealedSecrets integration
- [x] 24-04-PLAN.md -- Gap closure: fix ROADMAP misalignment on success criterion #5

### Phase 25: Application Manifests and MassTransit Transport
**Goal**: ApiService, Gateway, and Web are deployed via Kustomize and communicate correctly using K8s DNS and RabbitMQ messaging
**Depends on**: Phase 24
**Requirements**: MFST-01, MFST-02, MFST-03, MFST-04, MFST-05, MFST-06, TRAN-01, TRAN-02
**Success Criteria** (what must be TRUE):
  1. The storefront loads in a browser via the Gateway NodePort — browsing products and adding to cart works end-to-end
  2. Checkout completes successfully using the RabbitMQ transport (saga orchestrates stock reservation and mock payment)
  3. All pods report liveness and readiness as healthy (`kubectl get pods -n micro-commerce` shows all Running/Ready)
  4. All resources are in the `micro-commerce` namespace and have CPU/memory resource limits set
  5. Setting `MASSTRANSIT_TRANSPORT=AzureServiceBus` (Aspire dev path) and `RabbitMQ` (K8s path) both produce working configurations
  6. ApiService startup probe prevents liveness failure during slow first-boot EF Core migration (deferred from Phase 24 INFRA-05)
**Plans**: 3 plans
- [x] 25-01-PLAN.md -- MassTransit RabbitMQ transport switching, health endpoint fix, conditional Azure SDK
- [x] 25-02-PLAN.md -- ApiService, Gateway, Web K8s Deployment/Service manifests, Kustomize base/overlay
- [x] 25-03-PLAN.md -- Bootstrap script extension with app image build, kind load, and full stack deployment

### Phase 25.1: Update UI based on pencil design with shadcn (INSERTED)

**Goal:** Refresh all storefront and admin UI screens to match the split per-page pencil design files using existing shadcn/ui primitives and Tailwind CSS. Visual update only -- no new backend features, API endpoints, or routes.
**Requirements**: UI-THEME, UI-HOME, UI-CATALOG, UI-PRODUCT-DETAIL, UI-CART, UI-CHECKOUT, UI-ORDER-CONFIRM, UI-ORDER-HISTORY, UI-PROFILE, UI-AUTH-LOGIN, UI-ADMIN
**Depends on:** Phase 25
**Plans:** 12/12 plans complete

Plans:
- [x] 25.1-01-PLAN.md -- Theme/globals: CSS variable color palette + DM Sans font swap
- [x] 25.1-02-PLAN.md -- Homepage: hero, category pills, product cards, header, footer
- [x] 25.1-03-PLAN.md -- Product Catalog: sidebar filters, product grid, pagination
- [x] 25.1-04-PLAN.md -- Product Detail: image gallery, tabs, quantity stepper, reviews
- [x] 25.1-05-PLAN.md -- Shopping Cart: item rows, cart summary sidebar
- [x] 25.1-06-PLAN.md -- Checkout: step indicator, shipping/payment forms, order sidebar
- [x] 25.1-07-PLAN.md -- Order Confirmation: success banner, order details
- [x] 25.1-08-PLAN.md -- Order History: order list table, detail with status stepper
- [x] 25.1-09-PLAN.md -- User Profile: account sidebar, profile form, addresses
- [x] 25.1-10-PLAN.md -- Auth Login: branded Keycloak login page
- [x] 25.1-11-PLAN.md -- Admin Dashboard: dark sidebar layout, stat cards, tables
- [x] 25.1-12-PLAN.md -- Gap closure: wire ProductCard Add to Cart to useAddToCart hook

### Phase 26: ArgoCD GitOps
**Goal**: ArgoCD manages all cluster resources from Git using app-of-apps, replacing manual kubectl apply
**Depends on**: Phase 25
**Requirements**: GOPS-01, GOPS-02
**Success Criteria** (what must be TRUE):
  1. ArgoCD UI is reachable in the kind cluster and shows all services as Synced and Healthy
  2. Deleting a deployment manually causes ArgoCD to automatically restore it within one sync cycle
  3. The app-of-apps root Application manages each service as an independent child Application
**Plans**: 2 plans
- [ ] 26-01-PLAN.md -- ArgoCD manifests, child Applications, Kustomize per-service restructure
- [ ] 26-02-PLAN.md -- Bootstrap script ArgoCD integration and GitOps handoff

### Phase 27: CI/CD GitOps Loop Closure
**Goal**: A push to master automatically flows through CI image build, Git tag commit, and ArgoCD cluster rollout without manual intervention
**Depends on**: Phase 26
**Requirements**: CICD-02
**Success Criteria** (what must be TRUE):
  1. After a push to master, a new pod with the updated SHA-tagged image is running in the cluster within 5 minutes of the CI workflow completing
  2. Every deployment is traceable to a Git commit — the image tag in the overlay matches the commit SHA that triggered the build
**Plans**: TBD

### Phase 28: Observability
**Goal**: Traces, metrics, and logs from all cluster services are visible in the Aspire Dashboard without requiring .NET Aspire AppHost
**Depends on**: Phase 27
**Requirements**: OBSV-01, OBSV-02
**Success Criteria** (what must be TRUE):
  1. The Aspire Dashboard UI is reachable in the kind cluster and shows live traces from ApiService and Gateway
  2. A completed checkout flow produces a distributed trace visible end-to-end in the Dashboard spanning ApiService, the saga, and RabbitMQ consumers
  3. OTEL Collector pod stays healthy under normal load (no OOM kills, no dropped telemetry in logs)
**Plans**: TBD

## Progress

**Execution Order:** 23 → 24 → 25 → 26 → 27 → 28

## PLANNING COMPLETE

**Phase:** 26-argocd-gitops
**Plans:** 2 plan(s) in 2 wave(s)

### Wave Structure

| Wave | Plans | Autonomous |
|------|-------|------------|
| 1 | 26-01 | yes |
| 2 | 26-02 | yes |

### Plans Created

| Plan | Objective | Tasks | Files |
|------|-----------|-------|-------|
| 26-01 | ArgoCD manifests (root app, 6 child apps, NodePort, insecure CM) + Kustomize restructure for per-service management | 2 | 17 files (ArgoCD dir + kind-config + base kustomizations + per-service overlays) |
| 26-02 | Update bootstrap.sh to install ArgoCD v3.3.2 and replace manual kubectl apply with GitOps root app | 1 | 1 file (bootstrap.sh) |

### Key Design Decisions

1. **Per-service child Applications** -- Each of the 6 services (postgres, rabbitmq, keycloak, apiservice, gateway, web) gets its own ArgoCD Application, satisfying success criterion #3
2. **Sealed-secret.yaml removed from Kustomize resources** -- Infrastructure base kustomization.yaml files no longer reference sealed-secret.yaml (which doesn't exist in Git). Bootstrap applies sealed secrets directly via `kubectl apply -f`
3. **Per-service overlay directories** -- Created `overlays/dev/apiservice/`, `overlays/dev/gateway/`, `overlays/dev/web/` so each app service child Application can independently render with correct image tags. Also prepares for Phase 27 CI per-service image tag updates
4. **Infra apps point to base dirs, app services to per-service overlays** -- Infrastructure services need no image patching (point to `base/{service}/`), app services need image tags (point to `overlays/dev/{service}/`)
5. **ArgoCD v3.3.2** -- Per locked project decision (v3.3.0/3.3.1 have client-side apply migration bug)

### Requirement Coverage

| Requirement | Plans |
|-------------|-------|
| GOPS-01 | 26-01, 26-02 |
| GOPS-02 | 26-01, 26-02 |

| 26-01 | ArgoCD manifests (root app, 6 child apps, NodePort, insecure CM) + Kustomize restructure for per-service management | 2 | 17 files (ArgoCD dir + kind-config + base kustomizations + per-service overlays) |
| 26-02 | Update bootstrap.sh to install ArgoCD v3.3.2 and replace manual kubectl apply with GitOps root app | 1 | 1 file (bootstrap.sh) |

### Key Design Decisions

1. **Per-service child Applications** -- Each of the 6 services (postgres, rabbitmq, keycloak, apiservice, gateway, web) gets its own ArgoCD Application, satisfying success criterion #3
2. **Sealed-secret.yaml removed from Kustomize resources** -- Infrastructure base kustomization.yaml files no longer reference sealed-secret.yaml (which doesn't exist in Git). Bootstrap applies sealed secrets directly via `kubectl apply -f`
3. **Per-service overlay directories** -- Created `overlays/dev/apiservice/`, `overlays/dev/gateway/`, `overlays/dev/web/` so each app service child Application can independently render with correct image tags. Also prepares for Phase 27 CI per-service image tag updates
4. **Infra apps point to base dirs, app services to per-service overlays** -- Infrastructure services need no image patching (point to `base/{service}/`), app services need image tags (point to `overlays/dev/{service}/`)
5. **ArgoCD v3.3.2** -- Per locked project decision (v3.3.0/3.3.1 have client-side apply migration bug)

|-------------|-------|
| GOPS-01 | 26-01, 26-02 |

   101→  <action>
   102→    Rewrite the bootstrap script to integrate ArgoCD installation and hand off resource management. The new flow:
   103→
   104→    **Steps 1-2: KEEP UNCHANGED** (kind cluster creation, kubectl context)
   105→
   106→    **Steps 3-4: KEEP UNCHANGED** (SealedSecrets controller install + wait)
   107→
   108→    **Step 5: KEEP UNCHANGED** (create namespace)
   109→
   110→    **Step 6: Seal dev secrets -- MODIFY the seal_secret calls.** Keep the seal_secret function and all 3 seal_secret calls (postgres, rabbitmq, keycloak) exactly as-is. After all 3 secrets are sealed, add explicit kubectl apply for each sealed-secret.yaml file:
   111→    ```bash
   112→    info "Applying sealed secrets..."
   113→    kubectl apply -f "$SCRIPT_DIR/base/postgres/sealed-secret.yaml"
   114→    kubectl apply -f "$SCRIPT_DIR/base/rabbitmq/sealed-secret.yaml"
   115→    kubectl apply -f "$SCRIPT_DIR/base/keycloak/sealed-secret.yaml"
   116→    ```
   117→    WHY: sealed-secret.yaml is no longer referenced in base kustomization.yaml files (removed in Plan 01), so they must be applied explicitly. These must exist in the cluster before ArgoCD syncs the Deployments that reference the Secrets.
   118→
   119→    **Step 7: REMOVE** the old `kubectl apply -k "$SCRIPT_DIR/base/"` line. ArgoCD will handle this now.
   120→
   121→    **Step 8: REMOVE** the old infra pod wait commands. ArgoCD sync handles deployment.
   122→
   123→    **NEW Step 7: Install ArgoCD v3.3.2**
   124→    ```bash
   125→    # --- Step 7: Install ArgoCD ---
   126→    ARGOCD_VERSION="v3.3.2"
   127→    info "Installing ArgoCD ${ARGOCD_VERSION}..."
   128→    kubectl create namespace argocd --dry-run=client -o yaml | kubectl apply -f -
   129→    kubectl apply -n argocd --server-side --force-conflicts \
   130→      -f "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/argoproj/argo-cd/${ARGOCD_VERSION}/manifests/install.yaml"
   131→    ```
   132→    Use `--server-side --force-conflicts` because ArgoCD CRDs exceed 256KB annotation limit for client-side apply. Use idempotent namespace creation same as Step 5 pattern.
   133→
   134→    **NEW Step 8: Wait for ArgoCD to be ready**
   135→    ```bash
   136→    info "Waiting for ArgoCD to be ready..."
   137→    kubectl rollout status deployment argocd-server -n argocd --timeout=120s
   138→    kubectl rollout status deployment argocd-repo-server -n argocd --timeout=120s
   139→    kubectl rollout status deployment argocd-applicationset-controller -n argocd --timeout=120s
   140→    ```
   141→
   142→    **NEW Step 9: Configure ArgoCD for local access**
   143→    ```bash
   144→    info "Configuring ArgoCD for local access..."
   145→    kubectl apply -f "$SCRIPT_DIR/argocd/argocd-cmd-params-cm.yaml"
   146→    kubectl rollout restart deployment argocd-server -n argocd
   147→    kubectl rollout status deployment argocd-server -n argocd --timeout=120s
   148→    kubectl apply -f "$SCRIPT_DIR/argocd/argocd-server-nodeport.yaml"
   149→    ```
   150→    Apply the insecure ConfigMap, restart argocd-server to pick it up, wait for it to be ready, then apply the NodePort service.
   151→
   152→    **Steps 10-11: KEEP as-is** (build app images, load into kind). Renumber to Step 10 and Step 11.
   153→
   154→    **NEW Step 12: Apply ArgoCD root application**
   155→    ```bash
   156→    info "Applying ArgoCD root app-of-apps..."
   157→    kubectl apply -f "$SCRIPT_DIR/argocd/root-app.yaml"
   158→    ```
   159→    This triggers ArgoCD to discover and sync all child Applications, which in turn deploy all infrastructure and application manifests from Git.
   160→
   161→    **NEW Step 13: Wait for ArgoCD to sync all applications**
   162→    ```bash
   163→    info "Waiting for ArgoCD to sync all applications..."
   164→    # Wait for all child apps to be created by the root app
   165→    sleep 10
   166→
   167→    # Wait for infrastructure pods
   168→    info "Waiting for PostgreSQL..."
   169→    kubectl wait --for=condition=ready pod -l app=postgres -n "$NAMESPACE" --timeout=120s
   170→
   171→    info "Waiting for RabbitMQ..."
   172→    kubectl wait --for=condition=ready pod -l app=rabbitmq -n "$NAMESPACE" --timeout=120s
   173→
   174→    info "Waiting for Keycloak (may take up to 3 minutes for realm import)..."
   175→    kubectl wait --for=condition=ready pod -l app=keycloak -n "$NAMESPACE" --timeout=180s
   176→
   177→    # Wait for application pods
   178→    info "Waiting for ApiService (may take up to 3 minutes for first-boot migrations)..."
   179→    kubectl wait --for=condition=ready pod -l app=apiservice -n "$NAMESPACE" --timeout=180s
   180→
   181→    info "Waiting for Gateway..."
   182→    kubectl wait --for=condition=ready pod -l app=gateway -n "$NAMESPACE" --timeout=120s
   183→
   184→    info "Waiting for Web..."
   185→    kubectl wait --for=condition=ready pod -l app=web -n "$NAMESPACE" --timeout=120s
   186→    ```
   187→    Keep pod wait commands because the user needs to know when everything is ready. ArgoCD sync triggers deployment, but we still wait for pods to be Running.
   188→

    58→### 🚧 v3.0 Kubernetes & GitOps (In Progress)
    59→
    60→**Milestone Goal:** Deploy the full MicroCommerce stack to a kind-based Kubernetes cluster using GitOps via ArgoCD, with GitHub Actions CI building and pushing images to ghcr.io, Kustomize managing manifests, and Sealed Secrets handling credentials safely in Git.
    61→
    62→- [x] **Phase 23: Dockerfiles and Container Image Pipeline** - Build production-ready images for all three services and push them to ghcr.io via GitHub Actions (completed 2026-02-25)
    63→- [x] **Phase 24: Infrastructure Manifests and Secrets** - Deploy PostgreSQL, RabbitMQ, and Keycloak in the kind cluster with Sealed Secrets for all credentials (completed 2026-02-26)
    64→- [x] **Phase 25: Application Manifests and MassTransit Transport** - Deploy ApiService, Gateway, and Web with Kustomize base/overlay structure and RabbitMQ transport support (completed 2026-02-26)

    66→- [ ] **Phase 27: CI/CD GitOps Loop Closure** - Extend CI to commit SHA image tags back to the overlay, completing the full GitOps loop
    67→- [ ] **Phase 28: Observability** - Deploy OTEL Collector and standalone Aspire Dashboard for in-cluster monitoring

    71→### Phase 23: Dockerfiles and Container Image Pipeline
    72→**Goal**: Three production-ready container images exist in ghcr.io and are built automatically on every push to master
    73→**Depends on**: Phase 22 (v2.0 complete)
    74→**Requirements**: CONT-01, CONT-02, CONT-03, CONT-04, CICD-01
    75→**Success Criteria** (what must be TRUE):
    76→  1. `docker build` succeeds for ApiService, Gateway, and Web with no warnings or root-user violations
    77→  2. `docker run` starts each service and the process responds to health checks within 30 seconds
    78→  3. A push to master triggers the GitHub Actions workflow and all three images appear in ghcr.io tagged with the commit SHA
    79→  4. Image sizes are reasonable: .NET images use chiseled runtime and Next.js uses standalone output with node:alpine
    80→**Plans**: TBD

    82→### Phase 24: Infrastructure Manifests and Secrets
    83→**Goal**: PostgreSQL, RabbitMQ, and Keycloak run stably in the kind cluster with all credentials sealed in Git
    84→**Depends on**: Phase 23
    85→**Requirements**: INFRA-01, INFRA-02, INFRA-03, INFRA-04, INFRA-05, GOPS-03
    86→**Success Criteria** (what must be TRUE):
    87→  1. `kind create cluster --config kind-config.yaml` creates a cluster and all infrastructure pods reach Running state
    88→  2. PostgreSQL data persists across pod restarts (PVC-backed StatefulSet)
    89→  3. Keycloak admin UI is reachable and the MicroCommerce realm is pre-loaded from a ConfigMap
    90→  4. No plaintext credentials exist in any committed YAML file; all secrets are SealedSecret objects
    91→  5. Keycloak startup probe is configured with sufficient timeout (15s delay + 60s probing window) to survive slow first-boot realm import
    92→**Plans**: 4 plans
    93→- [x] 24-01-PLAN.md -- Kind cluster config, Kustomize scaffold, PostgreSQL StatefulSet
    94→- [x] 24-02-PLAN.md -- RabbitMQ Deployment, Keycloak Deployment with realm import
    95→- [x] 24-03-PLAN.md -- Bootstrap script with SealedSecrets integration
    96→- [x] 24-04-PLAN.md -- Gap closure: fix ROADMAP misalignment on success criterion #5

    99→**Goal**: ApiService, Gateway, and Web are deployed via Kustomize and communicate correctly using K8s DNS and RabbitMQ messaging
   100→**Depends on**: Phase 24
